Patch Set 2:

well, that's the point - to change OFD to call dt_lock() after dt_trans_start() ?

Patch Set 2:

Seems not, otherwise, without the dt_lock protection, someone may truncate the object (and related pages map) before the ofd_commitrw_write() start the transaction.

Patch Set 2:

in practice this can't happen due to LDLM ?

Patch Set 2:

I am not sure, if yes, then dt_lock is some over-functioned with LDLM.

Patch Set 2:

well, it's not as in many places we use OSD locking to serialize different stuff like attr_set, etc.
plus it provides us with a solid framework to build local services like llog.

Patch Set 2:

So for this case - OST write, with LDLM, do we still need dt_lock after transaction start in ofd_commitrw_write()?

Patch Set 2:

I'd leave it there for a while, just to make less assumptions. but the question is reasonable.

How to guarantee the transaction semantics, means that the main transaction has been done successfully, but the truncate transaction failed? From the upper layer view, the logic after truncate is done, but the truncate failed.

Patch Set 10:

Tests uploaded by Trevis Autotest2 from trevis-33vm1 for review-dne-part-1 received by maloo, run on CentOS Linux 7/x86_64: (https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sessions/6600a632-f42a-4a69-91dd-43549ad3f088). Ran 7 tests.  1 tests failed: sanity.

Patch Set 10:

What about reviving the async truncate patches for ext4?  That sped up deletes for block-mapped files (which could still help on the MDT) and may also be useful for extent-mapped files to get the block freeing out of the critical path.

The most recent version of the patch that I could find was for 2.4.24:

https://git.hpdd.intel.com/?p=fs/lustre-release.git;a=blob;f=lustre/kernel_patches/patches/ext3-delete_thread-2.4.24.patch;h=6059aba59458f3433d288dc0d77c1d4d8c702551;hb=1a00f9eeefdd2e6f738650fce8108c4da6eec8f2

Patch Set 10:

> How to guarantee the transaction semantics, means that the main transaction has been done successfully, but the truncate transaction failed? From the upper layer view, the logic after truncate is done, but the truncate failed.
few points can be made here:
1) majority of truncate in ZFS delayed to commit, it's considered OK
2) the most important part of truncate (i_size update) is part of a main transaction
3) we used to have async truncate in the past

Patch Set 10:

> What about reviving the async truncate patches for ext4?
benefits over doing this in OSD ?

Patch Set 10:

> > What about reviving the async truncate patches for ext4?
 > benefits over doing this in OSD ?

If we can get it landed upstream it will benefit everyone, and less code to maintain.  Since it doesn't affect on-disk format it should be easier to accept.

commit.subject: LU-10048 osd: async truncate
commit.message:
LU-10048 osd: async truncate

osd-ldiskfs should execute truncate outside of main transaction
handle. This avoids restarting truncate transaction handles in
main transaction, and allows "transaction first, locking second"
model on OST.
